title: HUF Collective Distribution Report — April 5, 2026 (PM): Conference Alignment and Validation Roadmap
distribution: All collective participants (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, Copilot, Peter)
supersedes: None. Extends CRPT-008 (April 5 AM: The Governance Scale Session).
purpose: This session integrated ChatGPT's conference review into action: created the Conference Core Stack (material triage PDF), enforced three alignment sentences across all conference-facing artifacts, updated abstract to v3 (197 words, three diagnostics, 25 errors, CR results, Backblaze), expanded presentation script to 16 slides, reorganised the codawork-2026 folder into phased subfolders, and produced a two-month validation roadmap prioritising the four weaknesses that could end the Coimbra conversation before it starts.

items_resolved_from_crpt_008_open_items.was: abstract_v2.md references 17 error sources and dual metric
items_resolved_from_crpt_008_open_items.now: abstract_v3.md: 197 words, three diagnostics named, CR results (mean 0.58, 31% invisible structural), Backblaze cross-domain sentence, 25-error catalogue. All three alignment sentences present.

items_resolved_from_crpt_008_open_items.was: 14 slides, no CR results, no Backblaze, 17-error count
items_resolved_from_crpt_008_open_items.now: 16 slides. New Slide 10 (CR results, Egozcue-Greenacre question answered). New Slide 11 (Backblaze cross-domain). Error count 17→25. Closing and elevator pitch rewritten with all three alignment sentences.

items_still_open_from_crpt_008.approach: Simulation study: generate compositions with known coupling structure, compute ad hoc CR, verify it tracks known coupling. More tractable than information-theoretic CR in timeline.
Sensitivity sweep: vary each threshold, count patterns, check if 31% STRUCTURAL is robust.
Compute 3x3 Spearman and Pearson correlation matrix for TV, Aitchison, CR across 74 EMBER + 35 Backblaze transitions. If r > 0.7 for any pair, n must be downgraded.
Design one alternative SBP (Dispatchable vs Intermittent at root). Rerun CR. Compare pattern distributions.

conference_core_stack.description: One-page PDF handout: material triage for CoDaWork 2026. Three zones: Front Room (scientific claim, framing, three diagnostics, cases, the ask), Second Room (CGS, 25-error catalogue, Ramsar, analyzer), Off Table (Peterson, philosophy, commercial). Three alignment sentences in boxed section. Footer: governance state.
conference_core_stack.origin: ChatGPT reviewed CRPT-008 and the full codawork-2026 folder. Produced the Front Room / Second Room / Off Table framework. Peter: 'wow, we got something useful going.' Claude built as PDF.
conference_core_stack.significance: This is the pocket reference for Coimbra. Prevents the most likely failure mode: leading with CGS or philosophy when the room wants the coherence residual.

three_alignment_sentences.description: Every conference-facing artifact now says the same three things:
sentences: This is a calibration study of a compositional monitoring instrument, not a finished framework.
The instrument uses three diagnostics: total variation distance, Aitchison distance, and the coherence residual.
A 25-error calibration catalogue with detection tests and governance actions makes the instrument falsifiable.
three_alignment_sentences.significance: ChatGPT identified that abstract, packet, union memo, analyzer, and talk script were saying different things about error count (17 vs 25) and diagnostic count (dual vs three). Now unified.

folder_restructure.description: codawork-2026/ reorganised from 46 flat files into phased structure:

codawork-2026/.description: Coimbra core — 27 files. The science, the presentation, the defense.

codawork-2026/post-coimbra/.description: Second Room and future deployment — 11 files. Real and important but not for first contact.

codawork-2026/archive/.description: Superseded versions, historical documents, Off Table — 8 files.

folder_restructure.significance: A reviewer opening the codawork-2026 folder now sees 27 focused conference files instead of 46 undifferentiated documents. The signal-to-noise ratio just doubled. Post-Coimbra material is preserved and accessible but does not clutter the core review.

two_month_roadmap.description: Strategic validation roadmap April 6 – June 5, 2026. Ten ranked weaknesses across three threat tiers. Week-by-week calendar with exit criteria. Depth vs width decision framework. Honest achievability assessment.
tier_1_could_lose_the_room: W-2: CR method has no theoretical grounding (Week 2 — simulation)
W-3: Thresholds are arbitrary (Week 1 — sensitivity sweep)
W-4: SBP sensitivity unanalyzed (Week 1 — alternative SBP)
tier_2_could_undermine_over_coffee: W-5: Zero human domain expert review (Week 3 — one email)
W-6: No ecological data ever processed (Week 3 — pilot dataset)
W-7: Backblaze has no writeup (Week 2 — documentation)
tier_3_strategic: W-9: CGS levels defined not derived
two_month_roadmap.calendar_summary: Week 1: kill the killers (independence, thresholds, SBP). Week 2: ground the CR (simulation). Week 3: seek width (ecological pilot, human review). Week 4: consolidate. Weeks 5-6: fork based on results. Weeks 7-8: presentation polish only.
two_month_roadmap.what_not_to_do: No more documentation. No CGS refinement. No Peterson follow-up. No analyzer UI polish. No new error sources. No scaling implementation. The time for cataloguing is over. The time for testing is now.
two_month_roadmap.significance: First time the project has a prioritised validation plan. The roadmap applies HUF-GOV doctrine to HUF's own development: measure first, then decide.

documents_modified_this_session.change: 14→16 slides. Added CR results (Slide 10), Backblaze cross-domain (Slide 11). Error count 17→25. Closing and elevator pitch rewritten with three alignment sentences.

Opening paragraph: three alignment sentences. Section 3b added (CR as third diagnostic). Architecture diagram: dual-metric → three-diagnostic protocol. Deployment Phase 1: 17→25 errors, dual→three diagnostics.
S015 entry added. File count 310→313. Folder restructure noted.
11 files → post-coimbra/. 8 files → archive/. See folder_restructure section.

corpus_state.verified_by: Full repo scan, April 5, 2026. find . -type f excluding .git = 312.
corpus_state.count_correction: Previous counts from S009 onward were +1 due to FOR_SHARON.md deletion not being decremented. S014 had additional +1 overcount. All corrected in STAGING_MANIFEST. 312 is the verified ground truth.
corpus_state.note: Folder restructure moved 19 files into subfolders (no files deleted, all preserved).

governance_state.note: This session was alignment and planning, not new computation. Governance state advances when the independence test (W-1) and SBP sensitivity (W-4) are completed.

collective_review_instructions.for_all_reviewers: CRPT-008 covered the science (CR results, Backblaze, CGS). CRPT-009 covers the response to ChatGPT's review and the validation plan. No need to re-debate CRPT-008 findings — focus forward on the roadmap.
what_to_review: TWO_MONTH_ROADMAP.md — Is the priority ordering correct? Are we missing a weakness?
abstract_v3.md — Is 197 words tight enough? Is the CR result stated clearly?
CONFERENCE_CORE_STACK.pdf — Is the Front Room / Second Room / Off Table triage correct?
Folder restructure — Did the right files end up in the right subfolders?
Three alignment sentences — Are they the right three sentences for Coimbra?
what_not_to_review: CRPT-008 findings — already reviewed by ChatGPT, results integrated
CGS level definitions — Post-Coimbra, no changes needed now
Historical documents in archive/ — preserved for the record, not for review
collective_review_instructions.peter_direction: No need to debate the past. Review the revisions and the plan forward. Send back to the collective with these updates.

next_session_priorities.blocks: Everything — if diagnostics are correlated, the 3^n framework must be recalibrated

one_sentence: CRPT-009 turned ChatGPT's conference review into action: three alignment sentences enforced across all artifacts, abstract v3 written (197 words with CR results), presentation expanded to 16 slides, codawork-2026 reorganised from 46 flat files into phased subfolders (27 Coimbra core / 11 post-Coimbra / 8 archive), a two-month validation roadmap produced that prioritises the four computations that could end the Coimbra conversation before it starts, and all file counts corrected to verified 312 (FOR_SHARON.md deletion was never decremented from S009 onward).
